description_long: This is a large, double-edged battle axe with a dark metallic blade featuring an aggressive, angular design and a prominent lower beard. The blade is thick and robust, with a jagged, almost ornamental upper edge and a wide, sweeping lower edge, suggesting both decorative and functional intent. The axe head is mounted onto a long, cylindrical handle that appears to be made of wood or a composite material, reinforced with metallic bands and capped with gold-colored metal at both ends. The handle includes a textured grip section, likely for improved handling and control, and the overall construction suggests durability and weight. The color palette is dominated by black, gold, and dark brown, giving the axe a striking and somewhat ceremonial appearance. No visible markings or labels are present, and the design is reminiscent of fantasy or medieval weaponry rather than a standard utility tool.
description: A large, ornate double-edged battle axe with a dark, jagged blade and a long, reinforced handle featuring gold accents and a textured grip.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the axe, highlighting the wide, angular blade and the gold-capped handle.
description_view_1: Top-down view emphasizing the narrow profile of the blade and the pointed end of the axe head.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, displaying the blade's aggressive lower beard and the handle's details.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view focusing on the handle's end cap and the alignment of the blade with the shaft.
attribute_handle_type: Long, straight cylindrical handle with a textured grip section, likely wood or composite, reinforced with gold-colored metal caps at both ends.
attribute_blade_design: Double-edged, wide, and angular blade with a pronounced lower beard, jagged upper edge, and ornamental features; dark metallic finish.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with visible metallic reinforcement at the joint.
